✦ A note before we begin
Dear Reader,

"In this busy world and fully professional CV era — I wanted to have a small corner of mine where I can be myself."

You're about to read something a little different from the usual portfolio or LinkedIn profile. No bullet points dressed up as achievements, no carefully curated "professional summary." Just me — candid, curious, and a little bit complicated.

I work in CSR and development, I care deeply about people and communities, and I believe the best conversations happen when someone is willing to be honest about who they are — not just what they've done.

So here's my diary. My journey. My corner.

Excited to know about me? 📖

— Soham

Chapter One
Who am I?
That's me :)

I'm Soham Patil, originally from Solapur, Maharashtra. For the past two and a half years, I've been working in the development sector designing and running CSR programs, and more importantly, making sure they actually make a difference on the ground.

My academic journey started with Geography at SP College Pune — and that move alone changed something in me. Solapur to Pune was my first time in a metro city, and I won't pretend it wasn't overwhelming. But that's exactly what cracked me open. The sheer size of it, the diversity, the ideas floating around campus — it all hit differently when you're a kid from a small town experiencing it for the first time. Geography gave me the subject, but Pune gave me a lens. That city quietly planted seeds I didn't even know were growing until I found myself wanting to understand the world far beyond any textbook.

But Pune had already ruined me for the ordinary. So when it came to choosing what's next, I couldn't bring myself to take the "safe" route — the one everyone around me seemed to be taking. Something kept pulling me toward the harder questions. That's what led me to Azim Premji University, Bangalore for a Master's in Development. Another city, another leap — except this time I was chasing something more intentional. APU wasn't a prestigious brand name or a guaranteed pay cheque, it was a place built around asking uncomfortable questions about society, equity and change. And honestly, that felt more honest to who I was becoming than any conventional path ever could.

These days I'm with Reliance Foundation, working across communities in education, health, livelihoods, gender, and environment. And I'll be honest — no classroom, no matter how good, fully prepares you for this. The ground is messier, more human, and far more humbling than any framework you study. There are days the work shakes everything you thought you understood. But that's also what makes it real. You stop performing knowledge and start actually listening.

Outside of work, philosophy is how I decompress — it's the only thing that helps me make sense of everything I see on the ground. And if you catch me going unusually quiet, I'm probably somewhere deep in Marathi literature, back home in my head in the best way possible. Oh, and Game of Thrones — I'll die on that hill. Not just as a fan, but because that show understood power, politics and human nature better than most things I've formally studied.

📍 Solapur → Pune → Bangalore → Palghar  |  🌐 English · Hindi · Marathi

Chapter Two
My Journey
2015 – 2017
AD Joshi Junior College, Solapur
Science — Physics, Chemistry, Biology
The beginning. Science stream, curious mind, and a lot of questions about how the world actually works.
HSC
V.G. Shivdare College, Arts, Commerce & Science
Physics · Chemistry · Biology · English · Sanskrit · Geography
2018 – 2021
B.A. Geography (Major)
Sir Parashurambhau College (SP College), Savitribai Phule Pune University
Political Science & Economics as minors. Geography taught me to see the world through space, place, and people — not just maps.
2022 – 2024
M.A. Development
Azim Premji University, Bangalore
The real turning point. Two years of unlearning, questioning, and reimagining what development truly means — in policy, in practice, and in life.
Chapter Three
The Work I've Done
Reliance Foundation · Current
Associate Project Head – CSR
📍 Palghar, Maharashtra  |  June 2024 – Present
Leading multi-sector CSR programs under the Holistic Development Project. Managing ₹1.8+ crore worth of initiatives touching education, health, livelihoods, gender, and environment across Palghar district. Coordinating 6 NGO partners, mentoring a 15-member field team, and running large-scale campaigns like Swachhata Hi Sewa, Poshan Abhiyan, Gram Deep, and Kaushalya Deep.
10,000+ beneficiaries 290 SHGs 3,425 individuals 2,000+ women in health programs 10+ schools upgraded 5,000+ mobilized
Reliance Foundation
State Thematic Lead – Integrated Value Chain Management
📍 Nanded, Maharashtra  |  March – July 2025
Strengthening FPOs and SHG Federations through market linkages and digital/phygital tools. Promoting women entrepreneurship in agribusiness and micro-enterprise under the Bharat India Jodo (BIJ) program.
Reliance Foundation
Young Manager – Phygital Transformation
📍 Nanded (Naigaon, Biloli, Mudkhed)  |  June 2024 – March 2025
Designed a phygital membership growth framework and built a block-level digital collaboration platform for FPOs across 3 rural blocks under the BIJ program.
CFSC Actions Foundation
Assistant Program Manager
📍 Karad, Maharashtra  |  January – December 2023
Worked on Project TOP (Transformation of Politics) India. Designed the 5S Grassroots Leadership Curriculum — Self, Society, System, Skills, Starting-off. Created the GramSwaraj Knowledge Capsule content strategy. Hosted Top India Talks, interviewing 10+ changemakers. Trained 100+ fellows on governance, emotional intelligence, and campaign management.
Also along the way...
Eklavya India Foundation · Yavatmal
Mentor
Nov 2022 – Nov 2023
Mentored first-generation PG aspirants on SOP writing and scholarship navigation.
AKRSP-India · Sayla, Gujarat
Project Intern – HDFC Parivartan CSR
May – June 2023
Natural farming impact assessment under the FDP initiative. Facilitated engagement with 430 farmers.
Chapter Four
What I've Explored

Research, for me, is about asking the questions that don't have easy answers. Here are two I've sat with — and tried to understand.


01
"Challenges Faced by Elected Women Representatives in Gram Panchayats and the Effectiveness of Capacity-Building Trainings by YASHADA"
📍 Kolhapur · Sangli · Satara, Maharashtra  |  Women in Local Governance
02
"A Study of Gendered Experiences at Railway Stations"
📍 Azim Premji University, Bengaluru  |  Public Spaces · Gender & Society

Both projects are rooted in a belief that systems are never neutral — and that understanding lived experience is the first step toward changing anything.

Chapter Five
The Rest of Me

The parts that don't fit on a CV. But honestly, these are the parts that make everything else make sense.


📖
Favourite Book
Kosala
by Bhalchandra Nemade — A Marathi novel that asks what it means to belong, to resist, and to live honestly in a world that demands conformity.
🎬
Favourite Series
Game of Thrones
Power, morality, consequence — the show at its best is a masterclass in how systems corrupt, how idealism meets reality, and why good people lose.
💬
What I Love
Long conversations
About life philosophy, history, the way things are and why. Give me a good question and I could talk for hours.
🌿
Languages
Marathi · Hindi · English
I think in Marathi, argue in Hindi, and write in English. Each one unlocks a different version of me.
